title: Tsmeu-6 personal wheel bike

The '''Tsmeu-6 Personal Wheel Bike''' was a [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Separatist]] ground craft resembling a giant wheel, with four clawed legs hanging from the sides.

==Characteristics==
Originally created as all-terrain military reconnaissance vehicle and later adopted by civilians for racing, the Tsmeu-6 Personal Wheel Bike could roll on the ground at great speed, crushing enemy [[Soldier/Legends|soldiers]] in its path. The legs were engaged whenever the craft needed to climb steep cliffs, especially useful on the sinkhole-filled [[Planet/Legends|planet]] of [[Utapau/Legends|Utapau]].<ref name="Lord of War" />

==History==
[[File:Tsmeu6WheelBikeWalker-SWCTP.png|thumb|left|200px|Grievous's Tsmeu-6 in walking configuration]]
At least three-wheel bikes were stored in the [[Level 10/Legends|tenth level]] of [[Utapau/Legends|Utapau]] by the Separatist forces. [[General/Legends|General]] [[Grievous/Legends|Grievous]] modified his bike by replacing the passenger seat with an [[Nd-53 double laser cannon/Legends|Nd-53 double laser cannon]]. He used his bike while being pursued by [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]]—who had been sent on a specific mission to either capture or kill the General—on a [[Varactyl/Legends|varactyl]] named [[Boga/Legends|Boga]]. After a long chase through several tunnels, Obi-Wan got close and jumped onto Grievous's wheel bike. This made Grievous lose control and the wheel bike went into a spin. Both Kenobi and the General were thrown off, and the wheel bike rampaged over the edge of a platform, falling to its destruction.<ref name="ROTS">{{Film|III}}</ref>

The young [[Miner/Legends|miner]] [[Jann Tosh]] used a [[48 Roller wheel bike/Legends|48 Roller wheel bike]], which was a later model of this vehicle that was created near the end of the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]].<ref name="TLP">{{DroidsCite|The Lost Prince}}</ref>

==Behind the scenes==
The real-life counterpart to the wheel bike is the [[Wikipedia:Monowheel|Monowheel]].

For ''Episode III'', the wheel bike started off as a variety of fearsome vehicular concepts befitting the nasty droid general, such as included tank-like chariots. One idea was that the vehicle itself was an intelligent droid, mirroring Obi-Wan's faithful Boga as a "living" mount for General Grievous.<ref name="DB-Wheel bike">{{DB|vehicle|wheelbike|wheel bike}}</ref>
